jokes.text: "Wherefore by their fruits ye shall know them." - (crowd cheers)
jokes.setup: Roger Furlong quoting scripture at what appears to be a convention
jokes.punchline: The irony of Furlong, a vulgar political operative, sanctimoniously quoting the Bible
jokes.explanation: Perfect character irony - Furlong using Biblical language while being the most profane character sets up his hypocrisy beautifully

jokes.text: It's clear that I'm a man, isn't it? I haven't been carded for years. Like, three years.
jokes.setup: Jason defending his manhood in Catherine's speech preparation
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's only recently stopped being carded, undermining his claim to maturity
jokes.explanation: Great economic writing - the 'three years' detail perfectly deflates his attempt at maturity

jokes.text: You know, like living next to a highway.
jokes.setup: Describing how they tune out Karen's constant hedging
jokes.punchline: Comparing her to ambient highway noise
jokes.explanation: Clever comparison that captures the exhausting nature of dealing with indecisive people

jokes.text: It sounds like you've been kidnapped by the Taliban.
jokes.setup: Catherine's flat, emotionless delivery of her love for Jason
jokes.punchline: Selina's extreme comparison to a hostage situation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Selina's tendency toward extreme analogies and her impatience with Catherine's performance

jokes.text: Well, I am such a zucchini fan, I practically photosynthesize.
jokes.setup: Meeting with a zucchini farmer
jokes.punchline: Dan's over-the-top attempt to relate using plant biology
jokes.explanation: Shows Dan's tendency to oversell himself with pseudo-clever remarks

jokes.text: as crammed as a drug mule's poop pocket
jokes.setup: Describing Dan's extensive contact list
jokes.punchline: Extremely crude and specific comparison
jokes.explanation: Shocking imagery that's memorable but relies heavily on vulgarity for impact

jokes.text: You ever hear of Jonah Ryan? No. Old buddy of mine. We go way back.
jokes.setup: Dan claiming connections
jokes.punchline: The farmer never heard of Jonah (because he's nobody)
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Dan's desperation and Jonah's irrelevance

jokes.text: the only people left in DC work at CVS
jokes.setup: Explaining why it's hard to meet important people during convention
jokes.punchline: Reducing DC's remaining population to just CVS employees
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about how empty DC gets during major political events

jokes.text: All sciences are precise. That's what science means.
jokes.setup: Response to someone saying science isn't precise
jokes.punchline: Amy's pedantic correction delivered with complete seriousness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Amy - her need to be technically correct about everything

jokes.text: He's half dead. - Half dead? - Yeah, he's been half-acuted.
jokes.setup: Death row execution gone wrong
jokes.punchline: Mike's made-up word 'half-acuted' for a botched execution
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining 'executed' with the concept of being half-done

jokes.text: Like being decapitated but surviving. Happens to chickens a lot.
jokes.setup: Explaining what a botched execution is like
jokes.punchline: Casual comparison to chicken decapitation
jokes.explanation: Mike's tendency to make inappropriate comparisons with disturbing casualness

jokes.text: Wow, dead kids... That never sounds good unless you're a stressed-out single mom.
jokes.setup: Reaction to news about hacked data of dead children
jokes.punchline: Dark joke about when dead children would be a relief
jokes.explanation: Shocking dark humor that pushes boundaries while making a twisted observation about parenting stress

jokes.text: There's only one midget I'm gonna let tell me what to do, and I'm gonna go tell her that I'm done.
jokes.setup: Doyle being told he can't quit
jokes.punchline: Referring to Selina as 'the midget' while asserting independence
jokes.explanation: Shows Doyle's crude disrespect, though relies on physical mockery

jokes.text: What's she gonna do, pin a dick on Gary and give him the job?
jokes.setup: Arguing there's no time to replace him as VP
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of Gary being given male anatomy to qualify for VP
jokes.explanation: Bizarre and memorable visual that captures political gender dynamics absurdly

jokes.text: She's fucking ethnic! This is Latinageddon.
jokes.setup: Learning O'Brien's VP pick is Laura Montez
jokes.punchline: Selina's panicked coinage of 'Latinageddon'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant portmanteau that captures Selina's panic and casual racism in one invented word

jokes.text: I wish I was that guy on death row. Apparently he's vomiting black bile.
jokes.setup: Selina's despair over the VP situation
jokes.punchline: Preferring to be the dying prisoner, with grotesque detail
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing Selina's dramatics while calling back to earlier subplot

jokes.text: He gets sexy Mexi. What am I stuck with? Doyle? Steve Martin's boring older brother.
jokes.setup: Comparing her VP options to O'Brien's choice
jokes.punchline: Describing Doyle as Steve Martin's boring older brother
jokes.explanation: Sharp celebrity comparison that captures Doyle's bland energy perfectly

jokes.text: There is no way on God's green cock that you can get out on matter of principle.
jokes.setup: Doyle trying to quit on principle
jokes.punchline: Ben's creative vulgar expression
jokes.explanation: Ben's creative profanity that's both shocking and character-appropriate

jokes.text: he's been fondling Jonah's balls like he's trying to figure out what the prize inside is.
jokes.setup: Explaining Teddy's sexual harassment of Jonah
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to examining a mystery prize
jokes.explanation: Brilliant comparison that makes sexual harassment simultaneously disgusting and absurd

jokes.text: It's like Christmas except happy.
jokes.setup: Celebrating Doyle stepping down
jokes.punchline: Suggesting Christmas isn't actually happy
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - captures the cynical worldview in just five words

jokes.text: God, you'd think they could do that right. Death row... the clue is in the name.
jokes.setup: Learning the death row inmate survived again
jokes.punchline: Pointing out the obvious function implied by 'death row'
jokes.explanation: Dark observational humor about bureaucratic incompetence

jokes.text: Isn't that spooky? Not even remotely.
jokes.setup: Amy and Selina having same thought about Chung
jokes.punchline: Selina's flat dismissal of the 'coincidence'
jokes.explanation: Sharp deflation of Amy's attempt at cuteness

jokes.text: There's no way it could be on the fritz. Just doublecheck and see if it is.
jokes.setup: Dan claiming coffee machine is broken to avoid getting coffee
jokes.punchline: Contradicting himself immediately while maintaining the lie
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment showing Dan's desperation and poor lying skills

jokes.text: Eggnog latte if it's in season. Is it? No, it isn't. Okay, I'll have a skinny cap.
jokes.setup: Ordering coffee at what's clearly not a coffee shop
jokes.punchline: Immediately answering his own seasonal question and pivoting
jokes.explanation: Shows Richard's oblivious social skills and entitled assumptions

jokes.text: I'm the wheel-greaser. I'm the puppet master. A man of many nicknames. Poonslayer.
jokes.setup: Jonah describing his importance
jokes.punchline: Escalating from business metaphors to sexual boasting
jokes.explanation: Pure Jonah - delusional self-importance mixed with juvenile sexual bragging

jokes.text: She and I are closer than two fat guys in an elevator.
jokes.setup: Dan claiming closeness with Amy
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison involving obesity and confined spaces
jokes.explanation: Absurd comparison that's memorable but doesn't fully land logically

jokes.text: Wait, actually, it's long for Amy.
jokes.setup: Claiming he calls Amy 'Ames' for short
jokes.punchline: Realizing 'Ames' is longer than 'Amy'
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah stupidity - confidently wrong then correcting himself

jokes.text: I think you must have given the go-ahead for Pearl Harbor.
jokes.setup: What did I do in a past life to deserve Karen?
jokes.punchline: Suggesting past-life war crimes to explain current suffering
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation that shows Amy's wit and frustration with Karen

jokes.text: I had Chungmania mania.
jokes.setup: Recalling when they called his speech 'Chungmania'
jokes.punchline: Having mania about his own mania
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay showing Chung's self-satisfaction with his catchphrase

jokes.text: And my Bible. Although she is my Bible, so that's two Bibles.
jokes.setup: Needing to consult his wife and his Bible about the VP offer
jokes.punchline: Realizing he's double-counting by calling his wife his Bible
jokes.explanation: Shows Chung's earnestness and slight confusion about his own metaphors

jokes.text: Oh, that's nice. Fuck off, I'm busy.
jokes.setup: Pierce offering good wishes for finding a new VP
jokes.punchline: Ben's immediate pivot from politeness to hostility
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben - acknowledging politeness before brutal dismissal

jokes.text: 30 years as a senator and your asshole's getting more screen time than your face.
jokes.setup: Doyle's prostate problems being used as excuse for resignation
jokes.punchline: Vulgar observation about which body part gets media attention
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about political priorities and media coverage of health issues

jokes.text: I understand you've been twirling Jonah's balls like they're some kind of exotic stress reducer.
jokes.setup: Confronting Teddy about sexual harassment
jokes.punchline: Comparing ball-fondling to stress ball therapy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd comparison that makes sexual harassment somehow both serious and ridiculous

jokes.text: I wish someone would sexually assault my staff. And by staff I mean penis.
jokes.setup: Hearing about the sexual harassment situation
jokes.punchline: Crude wordplay on 'staff' meaning both employees and penis
jokes.explanation: Pure Furlong vulgarity but relies on obvious wordplay

jokes.text: which of us hasn't gotten off in some guy's junk? You know, just for a laugh?
jokes.setup: Defending the harassment
jokes.punchline: Claiming it's normal male bonding
jokes.explanation: Furlong's typical inappropriate escalation

jokes.text: And that goes double for fondling Jonah.
jokes.setup: Teddy's defense that everything was to serve Furlong
jokes.punchline: Including the sexual harassment
jokes.explanation: Cringe moment showing Teddy's complete lack of awareness about appropriate behavior

jokes.text: And you never know. Someday somebody might just... (imitates gunshot)
jokes.setup: Explaining the career path from VP to President
jokes.punchline: Casual assassination gesture
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Selina's willingness to joke about presidential assassination to make a point

jokes.text: The shitty, two-faced, fat fucking turd said no.
jokes.setup: Asked to elaborate on Chung's response
jokes.punchline: String of increasingly crude descriptors
jokes.explanation: Pure Selina rage but relies heavily on profanity escalation without much craft

jokes.text: I like Tom James and I hate everyone.
jokes.setup: Recommending Tom James for VP
jokes.punchline: Using his hatred of everyone as a recommendation metric
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ben line - using his misanthropy as a character reference is brilliant economy

jokes.text: He died. - Oh, finally, right? It's one less thing to worry about.
jokes.setup: Update on the death row prisoner
jokes.punchline: Selina's relief at his death as task completion
jokes.explanation: Dark callback showing Selina's complete prioritization of political convenience over human life

jokes.text: Actually, I have very little sense of taste. Hence the tie.
jokes.setup: Joke about his hamburger recipe
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission about his fashion sense
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor that acknowledges his own limitations

jokes.text: Not that you don't. Though people say you don't.
jokes.setup: Claiming he knows how to get things done
jokes.punchline: Accidentally insulting Selina while trying to compliment himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment of social awkwardness and accidental insult

jokes.text: Yeah, shoe polish and he never even shared.
jokes.setup: Olsen has a drinking problem
jokes.punchline: Suggesting he drinks shoe polish and is selfish about it
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about alcoholism with absurd detail about sharing

jokes.text: I guess we could all eat pussy all day long, too, right?
jokes.setup: Suggestion to go all-female ticket
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual escalation as reductio ad absurdum
jokes.explanation: Shows Selina's crude reductionism but relies heavily on shock value

jokes.text: Yeah, that's why we 86'd him, Mike.
jokes.setup: Mike noting Culverson is 86 years old
jokes.punchline: Using restaurant slang '86' (meaning eliminated) for his age
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that connects age with elimination using restaurant terminology

jokes.text: I am an Ameri-can with a merry plan. I subscribe to a 'whistle while you work' philosophy. (Blows)
jokes.setup: Tom James giving an overly enthusiastic speech
jokes.punchline: Actually whistling after the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Shows Tom James's earnest, almost cornball political style

jokes.text: Have you been sent from the future to destroy me? 'Cause it's working.
jokes.setup: Karen's frustrating indecisiveness
jokes.punchline: Sci-fi conspiracy theory about Karen's purpose
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation that captures frustration through absurd conspiracy thinking

jokes.text: I think that each candidate has merits and demerits. And I don't know my left butt cheek from my right butt cheek, but I believe in listening to both butt cheeks and then farting out my asshole mouth.
jokes.setup: Mocking Karen's non-committal speaking style
jokes.punchline: Escalating the parody to scatological extremes
jokes.explanation: Extended parody that captures Karen's indecisiveness through crude escalation

jokes.text: I have bitten my tongue so long, it looks like a dog's cushion.
jokes.setup: Amy's been holding back criticism
jokes.punchline: Specific gross imagery of damaged tongue
jokes.explanation: Great Amy line - specific gross imagery that feels earned

jokes.text: You have two settings... No decision and bad decision.
jokes.setup: Amy's rant about Selina's leadership
jokes.punchline: Binary reduction of Selina's decision-making abilities
jokes.explanation: Devastating and perfectly structured insult that captures incompetent leadership precisely

jokes.text: I wouldn't let you run a bath without having the Coast Guard and the fire department standing by
jokes.setup: Continuing the competence attack
jokes.punchline: Absurd safety precautions for basic tasks
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing incompetence through absurd safety requirements for simple tasks

jokes.text: You are the worst thing that has happened to this country since food in buckets and maybe slavery.
jokes.setup: Amy's ultimate insult buildup
jokes.punchline: Comparing Selina to KFC and slavery
jokes.explanation: Shocking escalation that ranks historical atrocities, with 'food in buckets' being unexpectedly specific

jokes.text: The fact that you are a woman means we will have no more women presidents because we tried one and she fucking sucked.
jokes.setup: Amy's final condemnation
jokes.punchline: Claiming Selina will ruin it for all women
jokes.explanation: Devastating Amy finale - cruel but perfectly crafted character moment

jokes.text: The fact that you are a woman means we will have no more women presidents because we tried one and she fucking sucked.
jokes.setup: Amy's final devastating attack
jokes.punchline: Claiming Selina will ruin it for all women
jokes.explanation: Brutal and cutting final blow that attacks both Selina personally and her historical legacy

jokes.text: Well, that was clearly about me. She's just one of those women who resents powerful women.
jokes.setup: Amy's devastating resignation speech
jokes.punchline: Selina's complete misunderstanding of what just happened
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Selina's narcissistic inability to process legitimate criticism

jokes.text: Like Evel Knieval. - Yeah. - No, better. Lovely Knovley.
jokes.setup: Comparing Tom James to Evel Knieval
jokes.punchline: Mike's made-up improved version 'Lovely Knovley'
jokes.explanation: Mike's attempt at clever wordplay that shows his limited creativity

jokes.text: He has one skeleton. You're looking at her.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Tom James having no skeletons in his closet
jokes.punchline: Selina identifying herself as his skeleton
jokes.explanation: Self-aware moment where Selina recognizes she's a political liability

jokes.text: I could murder and make a lampshade out of a Manhattan.
jokes.setup: Being offered a drink
jokes.punchline: Mixing murder imagery with cocktail desires
jokes.explanation: Dark humor mixing violence with cocktails shows Tom's edgier side

jokes.text: I could murder and make a lampshade out of a Manhattan.
jokes.setup: Offering him drinks
jokes.punchline: Violent metaphor for wanting a cocktail
jokes.explanation: Tom's charming over-elaboration

jokes.text: Madam President, I... I had a feeling that this was in the wind. Sure, okay. Yeah. I'm afraid, with deep regret... Oh, my God. That I'm going to have to accept.
jokes.setup: Tom's formal response seems like a rejection
jokes.punchline: The twist that he's accepting, not declining
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection that builds tension before the relief of acceptance

jokes.text: I'm afraid, with deep regret... I'm going to have to accept.
jokes.setup: Sounds like he's declining
jokes.punchline: Actually accepting with fake reluctance
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - builds real tension then releases it perfectly

jokes.text: This is the best announcement since all those pandas got pregnant.
jokes.setup: Celebrating the Tom James announcement
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to panda pregnancies
jokes.explanation: Mike's random comparison showing his odd frame of reference for good news

jokes.text: Your champagne's been laced with polonium. I mean, not really. It's just to lighten up the moment a little bit.
jokes.setup: Kent firing Karen
jokes.punchline: Making an assassination joke then immediately clarifying
jokes.explanation: Kent's awkward attempt at dark humor while delivering bad news

jokes.text: Well, you know, it's funny because we actually bumped teeth there.
jokes.setup: Crowd likes their kiss
jokes.punchline: Revealing it was actually awkward
jokes.explanation: Selina's typical oversharing
